{"tool": "python_exec", "arguments": {
    "code": "import math\n\
             # Slope of line AB is -3\n\
             a, b = -3, 0\n\
             # Distance of point A from y-axis is given\n\
             x_a = 0\n\
             # Use the point-slope form of the equation of a line to find x-coordinate of B\n\
             x_b = x_a - b / a * x_a\n\
             # Distance between points A and B is the length of hypotenuse of right triangle OAB\n\
             c = math.sqrt(math.pow(x_b, 2) + math.pow(abs(b), 2))\n\
             # Slope of line CD is 0\n\
             m_cd = 0\n\
             # Use the point-slope form of the equation of a line to find x-coordinate of D\n\
             x_d = x_a + m_cd * (y_c - y_a) / (1 + abs(m_cd))\n\
             # Length of side CE is given as 3\n\
             # Use the Pythagorean theorem to find the length of side BE\n\
             h = 3\n\
             b_e = math.sqrt(math.pow(c, 2) - math.pow(h / 2, 2))\n\
             # Area of quadrilateral OBEC is the sum of areas of triangles OAB and BOC\n\
             # Area of triangle OAB is 1/2 * base * height\n\
             # Base of triangle OAB is the length of side OB\n\
             base_oab = c\n\
             # Height of triangle OAB is the length of side OA\n\
             height_oab = abs(x_a)\n\
             area_oab = 0.5 * base_oab * height_oab\n\
             # Area of triangle BOC is 1/2 * base * height\n\
             # Base of triangle BOC is the length of side BC\n\
             base_boc = 6\n\
             # Height of triangle BOC is the length of side BE\n\
             height_boc = b_e\n\
             area_boc = 0.5 * base_boc * height_boc\n\
             # Total area of the quadrilateral\n\
             area = area_oab + area_boc"
}}

(Note: The provided Python code assumes that the `math` module is imported, which is already pre-imported in the tool.)
